brief_title: A Study in Ovarian Cancer Patients Evaluating Rucaparib and Nivolumab as Maintenance Treatment Following Response to Front-Line Platinum-Based Chemotherapy
official_title: ATHENA (A Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 Study in Ovarian Cancer Patients Evaluating Rucaparib and Nivolumab as Maintenance Treatment Following Response to Front-Line Platinum-Based Chemotherapy)
brief_summary: This is a Phase 3, randomized, multinational, double-blind, dual placebo-controlled, 4-arm study evaluating rucaparib and nivolumab as maintenance treatment following response to front-line treatment in newly diagnosed ovarian cancer patients. Response to treatment will be analyzed based on homologous recombination (HR) status of tumor samples.

interventions.name: Rucaparib
interventions.description: Oral rucaparib will be administered twice daily

interventions.name: Nivolumab
interventions.description: IV nivolumab will be administered once every 4 weeks

interventions.name: Placebo Oral Tablet
interventions.description: Placebo tablets will be administered twice daily

interventions.name: Placebo IV Infusion
interventions.description: IV placebo will be administered once every 4 weeks

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Newly diagnosed advanced (FIGO stage III-IV) epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer.
* Completed cytoreductive surgery, including at least a bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy and partial omentectomy, either prior to chemotherapy (primary surgery) or following neoadjuvant chemotherapy (interval debulking)
* Completed first-line platinum-based chemotherapy and surgery with a response, in the opinion of the Investigator
* Sufficient tumor tissue for planned analysis
* ECOG performance status of 0 or 1
* Patients must be 20 years of age to consent in Japan, Taiwan and South Korea; in all other participating countries patients must be 18 years of age to consent

Exclusion Criteria:

* Pure sarcomas or borderline tumors or mucinous tumors
* Active second malignancy
* Known central nervous system brain metastases
* Any prior treatment for ovarian cancer, other than the first-line platinum regimen
* Evidence of interstitial lung disease or active pneumonitis
* Active, known or suspected autoimmune disease
* Condition requiring active systemic treatment with either corticosteroids (\>10 mg daily prednisone equivalent) or other immunosuppressive medications

locations.facility: MD Anderson Cancer Center at Cooper

primary_outcomes.measure: Monotherapy Arm B and Arm D: Investigator Assessed Progression-free Survival (PFS)
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: From randomization until disease progression (up to the primary data analysis at approximately 39 months)
primary_outcomes.description:
PFS by investigator was defined as the time from randomization to disease progression, according to Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors (RECIST) version 1.1 as assessed by the investigator, or death due to any cause, whichever occurred first.

Progressive disease was defined as a 20% increase in the sum of the longest diameter of measurable lesions, an unequivocal increase in existing non-measurable lesion(s), or the appearance of unequivocal new lesion(s).

primary_outcomes.timeFrame: From randomization until disease progression (up to the primary data analysis at approximately 39 months)

primary_outcomes.measure: Combination Therapy Arm A and Arm B: Investigator Assessed PFS
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: From randomization until disease progression (up to the combination therapy interim analysis at approximately 66 months)
primary_outcomes.description:

collaborators: European Network of Gynaecological Oncological Trial Groups (ENGOT)
